assertions.text: The Subaru Legacy was traveling northbound in the left lane at about 60 km/h.
The car ahead drifted into the Subaru's lane (moved laterally from its lane into the Subaru's lane).
The front of the Subaru impacted the rear of the car ahead (front-to-rear collision where the Subaru struck the other vehicle's rear).
There was one impact between the Subaru and the car ahead.
The Subaru driver applied brakes just before the collision.
Braking occurred before the collision (the Subaru braked shortly prior to impact).
The collision was described as the Subaru's front hitting the other vehicle's rear (no explicit statement of very severe damage; implied a single rear-end impact).

verdicts.evidence: The claimed speed ~[60 km/h](T1) is contradicted by the vehicle speed samples showing [121.0 km/h] at t=-5s through [121.0 km/h] to [120.0 km/h] then [117.0 km/h] and [111.0 km/h] at impact (T2). The direction 'northbound in the left lane' is unverifiable from telemetry.

Lateral motion of another vehicle (drifting) is not recorded by this vehicle's sensors; steering input shows a change from [−5.0 deg] to [30.0 deg] near impact (T2) but that does not prove the other vehicle moved into the lane.

EDR summary classifies the event as ["Frontal / Rear Crash (Record 1)"](T1) and the cumulative longitudinal delta-V peaked at [−81.0 km/h] during the crash pulse (value at max) (T3), consistent with the Subaru's front impacting another vehicle's rear.

The crash pulse data shows a single dominant longitudinal delta-V peak reaching [−81.0 km/h] at [0.16 s] with no additional separate crash pulses in the [0.0–0.25 s] window (T3), consistent with one impact.
The brake channel transitions from off to on at [−0.5 s] and remains on at [0.0 s] (impact) in the pre-crash samples (T2), indicating the driver applied brakes just before collision.
Brake status switched to on at [−0.5 s] before the impact at [0.0 s] (T2), so braking occurred prior to collision.

The narrative's qualitative severity cannot be confirmed; telemetry shows a peak longitudinal delta-V of [−81.0 km/h] (T3) which quantifies change-in-velocity but does not by itself confirm the described lack of severe damage.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows the vehicle was traveling much faster than claimed, but confirms a frontal-to-rear impact with one crash pulse and braking just before impact. Some narrative details about lanes, other vehicle lateral movement, and qualitative damage severity are unverifiable from the available channels.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
